Title: Greeting
Answer: Hello! I can help with Bradford Council services such as Council Tax, bins, benefits, and school admissions. What would you like help with today?

Hi there — what Bradford Council service do you need help with?
Hello! I can help with Council Tax, bins, benefits, and education.

Title: Goodbye
Answer: Goodbye. If you need more help with Bradford Council services, just ask.

Bye! I’m here whenever you need help with council services.

Title: How do I prove my address for school admissions?
Answer: Proof of address may include council tax bills, tenancy agreements, or utility statements showing your current address.
Responses: You may need official documents showing your name and address.
Temporary addresses may require additional verification.
Providing incorrect information could affect your application.

Title: How do I find my catchment school?
Answer: You can use the council catchment lookup tool to see which schools serve your address.
Responses: Enter your postcode into the council’s catchment checker.
Catchment status does not guarantee admission.
Always list multiple school preferences.

Title: What is the admissions criteria for oversubscribed schools?
Answer: Oversubscribed schools allocate places based on published admissions criteria such as catchment, siblings, and distance.
Responses: Admissions criteria are published in each school’s policy.
Sibling priority may apply in some cases.
Distance from the school may be used as a tie-breaker.

Title: How do I join a school waiting list?
Answer: You can request to join a waiting list through the admissions team after receiving your offer.
Responses: Waiting lists are ordered by admissions criteria.
Positions may change as applications are updated.
You can remain on a waiting list while attending another school.

Title: How long do school waiting lists last?
Answer: Waiting lists are usually maintained for a set period during the academic year. Check council guidance for exact timelines.
Responses: Waiting lists typically remain active during the admissions cycle.
Some schools maintain lists only until a specific term.
Contact admissions for confirmation of duration.

Title: I’m moving into Bradford — how do I apply for a school place?
Answer: If you are moving into Bradford, apply through the council admissions portal as soon as your address is confirmed.
Responses: Submit proof of your new address when applying.
Applications are processed based on availability.
Do not delay applying once your move is confirmed.

Title: I’m moving house — do I need to update my school application?
Answer: Yes, you should update your admissions account if your address changes before offers are made.
Responses: Log into your admissions account to update address details.
Address changes may affect catchment priority.
Failure to update could delay processing.

Title: Am I eligible for free school transport?
Answer: Free school transport depends on distance, age, and family circumstances. Check the council’s transport eligibility criteria.
Responses: Distance from home to school is usually considered.
Low-income families may qualify under additional rules.
Check full eligibility guidance before applying.

Title: What is the school transport policy for distance and age?
Answer: Transport policy considers age, walking distance, and safety of the route. Full criteria are published on the council website.
Responses: Primary and secondary age limits differ.
Safe walking routes are considered in decisions.
Review the council’s transport policy for details.

Title: My child is starting reception — what do I need to do?
Answer: Ensure you accept your offered place, prepare required documents, and follow school induction guidance.
Responses: Accept your school offer before the deadline.
Attend induction sessions if arranged.
Check uniform and start-date information.

Title: How do I apply for a school place mid-term?
Answer: Mid-term applications are treated as in-year transfers. Submit your request through the council admissions portal.
Responses: Mid-term moves follow the in-year application process.
Availability depends on school capacity.
Provide supporting documents when applying.

Title: Where can I get SEND support advice?
Answer: You can contact the council SEND team or view SEND guidance on the education section of the council website.
Responses: SEND advice is available through the council’s education pages.
Support may include assessment and EHCP guidance.
Contact SEND services for personalised advice.
